decks.notes: The main concept of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to quickly deploy aggressive creatures like Llanowar Elves, Elvish Mystic, and Thalia, Guardian of Thraben while disrupting the opponent's plans with cards like Aven Interrupter and Archon of Emeria. The deck aims to maintain board presence with cards like Skyclave Apparition and protect itself with cards like Voice of Resurgence. Collected Company helps to flood the board with creatures and Elspeth, Storm Slayer provides a powerful finisher if the game goes longer. The deck focuses on efficient creatures and disruption to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.

The main concept of this Red Deck Wins aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a combination of low-cost creatures and burn spells. By efficiently using creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage to chip away at the opponent's life total and finishing them off with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Lightning Strike, the deck aims to win the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize. The deck also includes a few utility lands and cards like Ramunap Ruins and Den of the Bugbear to provide additional reach and resources.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent quickly with a combination of cheap, aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, backed up by burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage. The deck also focuses on tempo and card advantage through cards like Academic Dispute and Stock Up, while utilizing cards like Vivi Ornitier and Stormchaser's Talent for additional damage and card draw. The deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent while controlling the board and minimizing the opponent's options.

Focused on using control spells and card advantage to outlast opponents and disrupt their game plan, this UW Control deck aims to control the board with counterspells, removal, and card draw. Utilizing powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Elspeth, Storm Slayer, as well as versatile spells like Dovin's Veto and Supreme Verdict, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents to secure a win. The inclusion of Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion adds additional value and versatility to the deck.

This deck focuses on the synergy between Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II, utilizing various support cards to disrupt the opponent and protect the combo pieces. The main strategy revolves around generating value from sacrificing creatures, controlling the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse, and ensuring consistency with Thoughtseize. The mana base is designed to support the color requirements of the deck while also providing utility lands for additional benefits. Overall, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces quickly and efficiently to overwhelm the opponent.

This aggro deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with low-cost creatures and using cards like Collected Company to generate board presence. The deck combines the mana ramp of Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves with powerful creatures like Archon of Emeria and Skyclave Apparition to apply constant pressure. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben disrupts the opponent's game plan, while Boseiju, Who Endures and Ouroboroid provide resilience against removal spells. By efficiently deploying threats and disrupting the opponent's strategy, this Selesnya Aggro deck aims to control the pace of the game and secure a swift victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing powerful removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay to control the board while generating value with creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. The deck also includes disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Duress to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The mana base is designed to provide consistent access to both green and black mana sources, allowing for efficient casting of spells. The overall strategy is to out value the opponent and control the game until a win condition can be established.

This UW Control deck focuses on control and removal spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan while generating card advantage through draw spells and card filtering. The deck also utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Elspeth, Storm Slayer to gain incremental advantage and close out games. The inclusion of Kaheera as a companion adds consistency to the deck by providing an additional threat and value. The deck aims to outlast the opponent with board wipes and counter spells, while slowly grinding out a win condition through card advantage and powerful threats.

The deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo through cards like Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II. It utilizes a mix of removal, hand disruption, and mana fixing to protect and enable the combo. The strategy involves using cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the opponent's board while setting up the combo pieces. The deck aims to efficiently control the game until it can safely deploy the Greasefang Parhelion combo to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.

The deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful and difficult-to-stop win condition. The deck includes a variety of cards to help protect the combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's strategy, such as Fatal Push and Thoughtseize. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Iron-Shield Elf and Monument to Endurance to help sustain the combo and provide additional support. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble the combo and overwhelm the opponent with a powerful board presence.

The deck focuses on utilizing Lotus Field to generate a large amount of mana quickly, allowing for explosive plays such as casting multiple Scapeshifts in a single turn or playing powerful creatures ahead of schedule. The deck includes cards like Arboreal Grazer and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods to ramp up to Lotus Field faster, while also including cards like Aftermath Analyst and Morlun, Devourer of Spiders for additional value and protection. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a massive amount of resources and threats, leading to a swift victory through a well-timed combo.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing a combination of removal spells, hand disruption, and powerful creatures. The strategy is to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Duress, while also removing threats with Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay. The deck also includes strong creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to apply pressure and close out the game. The mana base is optimized to support the deck's strategy and ensure consistency in casting spells.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by disrupting the opponent's hand with cards like Thoughtseize and Duress, while also utilizing removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay to deal with threats on the board. The deck aims to establish a strong board presence with creatures like Llanowar Elves and Sentinel of the Nameless City, and eventually close out the game with powerful finishers like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse. It also includes utility lands like Boseiju, Who Endures and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth to further support its strategy.

The main focus of this UR Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm your opponent with low-cost aggressive creatures and burn spells. By combining fast creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage with burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage, you aim to steadily chip away at your opponent's life total. Experimental Synthesizer and Stormchaser's Talent provide additional card advantage and tempo, while cards like Boomerang Basics and Riverpyre Verge offer flexibility and utility. The deck aims to maintain pressure and control of the board to dominate the game and secure victory.

The main concept of this Aggro - Arclight Phoenix deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with spells while efficiently dealing with threats on the board. Utilizing cards like Consider, Sleight of Hand, and Opt to cycle through the deck and bring back Arclight Phoenixes from the graveyard. Ledger Shredder and Picklock Prankster help with graveyard manipulation, while cards like Fiery Impulse and Lightning Axe provide removal options. The deck focuses on getting Arclight Phoenixes back on the battlefield as quickly as possible while controlling the board with spells and creatures.

This UW Control deck focuses on utilizing Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion to create a longer game with extra value. The deck includes powerful control spells like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Dovin's Veto to manage the board and counter opponents' threats. Cards like No More Lies and Memory Deluge help maintain card advantage, while Field of Ruin and Hengegate Pathway provide mana fixing and disruption. The deck aims to control the game through counterspells, removal, and card advantage until it can win with powerful finishers like The Wandering Emperor or Hall of Storm Giants.
